output:
## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Brand audit (current-state assessment)
- Competitive landscape map
- Customer/stakeholder insight synthesis
- Positioning statement
- Messaging hierarchy (tagline, value props, proof points)
- Voice & tone guide
- Naming recommendation
- Visual identity direction (mood, not full system)
- Brand guide PDF (final artifact)
- Internal rollout deck / brand story narrative
- Agency/designer handoff brief

**Time blocks**
- 60-min kickoff
- 45-min stakeholder interview (per stakeholder)
- 60-min customer interview (per customer)
- 90-min positioning workshop
- 60-min messaging review session
- 60-min final presentation
- 30-min check-ins (weekly)

**Access tiers**
- Async-only (email, 48hr turnaround)
- Scheduled calls only
- Slack/WhatsApp during business hours
- Direct line + same-day response

**Support levels**
- 1 round of revisions on each deliverable
- Unlimited revisions within engagement window
- Post-delivery office hours (e.g., 2x 30-min over 60 days)
- 90-day async support window
- Quarterly check-ins for 12 months

**Customizations**
- # of stakeholder interviews (3 / 6 / 10)
- # of customer interviews (0 / 3 / 6)
- B2B vs. B2C vs. mixed
- Industry depth (generalist vs. embedded research)
- Internal-only vs. handoff-ready (designer/agency)
- Founder-led vs. team-led brand
- Refresh vs. ground-up

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Depth** | Research thoroughness (interviews, audit rigor) | Light (3 stakeholder, 0 customer) / Standard (6 stakeholder, 3 customer) / Deep (10 stakeholder, 6 customer) | Deep blocked on Sprint pace |
| **Breadth** | Brand-system scope | Verbal-only (positioning + messaging) / +Voice (adds voice/tone guide) / Full (adds visual direction + naming option) | Naming add-on requires Full; visual direction requires +Voice or higher |
| **Pace** | Calendar duration | Sprint (2 weeks) / Standard (6 weeks) / Extended (10 weeks) | Sprint blocks Deep depth and Full breadth |
| **Format** | Final artifact form | Working doc only / Branded PDF guide / PDF + rollout deck + handoff brief | Rollout deck requires Standard or Extended pace |
| **Support** | Post-engagement access | None / 60-day office hours / 90-day async + quarterly for 12mo | Quarterly support requires Standard or Extended pace |
| **Access** | During-engagement responsiveness | Scheduled calls only / Slack business hours / Direct line same-day | Direct line blocks Sprint pace |
| **Audience Mode** *(domain-specific)* | Who the brand work targets | Internal-only / Handoff-ready / Market-facing launch-ready | Market-facing requires Full breadth + rollout deck |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint pace + Deep depth
- Sprint pace + Full breadth
- Sprint pace + Direct-line access
- Naming add-on without Full breadth
- Rollout deck without Standard or Extended pace
- Quarterly 12-mo support without Standard or Extended pace
- Market-facing audience mode without Full breadth + rollout deck

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning line | Pricing logic |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Positioning Sprint** | Depth: Light · Breadth: Verbal-only · Pace: Sprint (2w) · Format: Working doc · Support: None · Access: Scheduled calls | For early-stage founders who need clarity on *what they stand for* before they spend on design or hiring. | **$5,500** — ~37% of flagship; entry point that converts "I can't articulate us" into a usable positioning statement. Anchored against the cost of bad messaging compounding for 6+ months. |
| **Core — Brand Guide** *(flagship, current offering)* | Depth: Standard · Breadth: Full · Pace: Standard (6w) · Format: Branded PDF · Support: 60-day office hours · Access: Slack business hours | For funded startups and growing companies ready to operationalize a brand across product, marketing, and hiring. | **$15,000** — your existing anchor. Senior-strategist-only delivery at roughly half the cost of comparable agency engagements ($25–40K). |
| **Premium — Brand System + Embed** | Depth: Deep · Breadth: Full + naming · Pace: Extended (10w) · Format: PDF + rollout deck + handoff brief · Support: 90-day async + quarterly 12mo · Access: Direct line · Audience: Market-facing | For Series A+ companies treating brand as infrastructure, often pre-rebrand or pre-launch. | **$32,000** — below consultancy retainer rates ($40–60K) and below sum-of-parts (Core $15K + naming $8K + extended research $6K + 12-mo embed $8K = $37K). Flagship discount preserved. |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

**Base price:** $5,500 (Lite configuration)

**Per-dimension upgrade pricing:**
- **Depth:** Light → Standard +$3,500 · Standard → Deep +$4,500
- **Breadth:** Verbal → +Voice +$1,800 · +Voice → Full +$2,800 · Naming add-on +$3,500
- **Pace:** Sprint → Standard +$1,200 · Standard → Extended +$2,000
- **Format:** Working doc → Branded PDF +$1,500 · PDF → PDF+deck+handoff +$2,500
- **Support:** None → 60-day +$1,200 · 60-day → 90-day+quarterly +$3,800
- **Access:** Scheduled → Slack +$1,500 · Slack → Direct line +$2,500
- **Audience Mode:** Internal → Handoff-ready +$1,500 · Handoff → Market-facing +$2,500

**Floor:** $5,500 (Lite as-is — anything less isn't a viable engagement)

**Ceiling:** $26,000 — above this, auto-route to Premium quote (custom scoping conversation)

**Configurator-enforced dependencies:** all invalid combinations from Part 2.

**Flagship-protection rule:**
- BYO sum to reach Core's exact configuration: $5,500 + $3,500 + $1,800 + $2,800 + $1,200 + $1,500 + $1,200 + $1,500 = **$19,000** vs. Core flagship **$15,000** → Core wins by $4,000 (21% discount).
- BYO sum to reach Premium's exact configuration: $5,500 + $3,500 + $4,500 + $1,800 + $2,800 + $3,500 + $1,200 + $2,000 + $1,500 + $2,500 + $1,200 + $3,800 + $1,500 + $2,500 + $1,500 + $2,500 = **$39,800** vs. Premium flagship **$32,000** → Premium wins by $7,800 (20% discount).

Both flagships remain the rational choice for buyers who land on those configurations.

---

Want me to take this further with any of these?
- **Configurator output mode** — re-emit Parts 2 & 3 as JSON/YAML for Typeform/Webflow/custom builder
- **Highest-leverage atom** — identify the 1–2 atoms that, if standardized (templates, intake forms, scripted workshops), unlock the most BYO velocity
- **Edge-case stress-test** — name a weird inbound (e.g., "solo founder, $4K budget, needs naming only") and I'll walk it through the configurator

*Assuming general 1-on-1 fitness coaching (training plans + check-ins, virtual or hybrid), not a specific sub-niche like physique competition prep or post-rehab strength; will re-anchor with $ figures once you name your sub-niche, average client price, and session/check-in cadence.*

---

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Initial assessment report (movement screen, goal-setting, baseline metrics)
- Custom training program (week-by-week, exercise prescription)
- Nutrition framework (macros, meal structure — not full meal plans unless specified)
- Progress report (bi-weekly or monthly check-in writeup)
- Form-check video review (async)
- Deload/recovery protocol
- Program revision (mid-cycle adjustment)

**Time blocks**
- 60-min onboarding/assessment call
- 30-min check-in call
- 45-min live virtual training session
- 60-min in-person training session
- 15-min "quick-fix" call (form, plateau, motivation)

**Access tiers**
- Async messaging (24-hr response, business hours)
- Async messaging (same-day response, 7 days)
- Direct phone/text access (always-on, capped per week)
- Shared training log review (weekly / daily)

**Support levels**
- Form-check video reviews (count per month: 2 / 4 / unlimited)
- Program adjustments (monthly / bi-weekly / on-demand)
- Habit/accountability check-ins (weekly written / weekly call / daily ping)

**Customizations**
- Goal type (fat loss / hypertrophy / strength / sport-specific / general health)
- Training environment (home / commercial gym / hybrid)
- Equipment access (bodyweight / minimal / full gym)
- Session frequency (3 / 4 / 5+ days)
- Nutrition depth (none / framework / tracked macros / full meal plan)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Depth | Assessment + program specificity | Template-based / Custom / Fully bespoke + biometrics | Bespoke requires monthly check-in minimum |
| Pace | Coaching cycle length | 4-week sprint / 12-week standard / 6-month transformation | Sprint blocks bespoke depth |
| Format | Session delivery | Async-only / Virtual live / In-person / Hybrid | In-person requires geographic match |
| Support | Check-in cadence + access | Monthly / Bi-weekly / Weekly / Daily | Daily requires direct-text access tier |
| Nutrition Scope | Food guidance level | None / Framework / Tracked macros / Full meal plan | Tracked macros require bi-weekly minimum |
| Accountability | Behavioral structure | Self-directed / Logged / Coached / Concierge | Concierge requires weekly+ support |
| Add-ons | One-offs | Form-check pack, deload protocol, travel program, photoshoot peak | — |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint pace + Fully bespoke depth (not enough cycles to justify)
- Async-only + Daily support (channel mismatch)
- In-person + clients outside service area
- Tracked macros + Monthly check-ins (data goes stale)
- Concierge accountability + anything below Weekly support

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

### Tier 1 — **Self-Drive** (Lite)
- **Configuration:** Template-based depth · 12-week pace · Async-only · Monthly support · Framework nutrition · Self-directed accountability
- **Positioning:** For self-motivated lifters who want a real coach's program and a monthly course-correction, not a hand-holder.
- **Pricing logic:** Anchored ~30–35% of flagship; priced above app-based coaching (Future, Caliber ≈ $200/mo) but below 1-on-1 norms. **+$A/month**

### Tier 2 — **Coached** (Core / flagship)
- **Configuration:** Custom depth · 12-week pace · Virtual live (2x/mo) + async · Bi-weekly support · Tracked macros · Coached accountability
- **Positioning:** For clients who need a custom program, real coaching contact, and nutrition that adjusts with them — the standard 1-on-1 experience, productized.
- **Pricing logic:** Anchored against your current 1-on-1 price (this *is* your current service, structured). Should price **below** the BYO sum-of-parts to reach this configuration. **+$B/month**

### Tier 3 — **Concierge** (Premium)
- **Configuration:** Bespoke depth + biometrics · 6-month pace · Hybrid (in-person + virtual) · Daily support · Full meal plan · Concierge accountability
- **Positioning:** For executives, athletes, or clients with a hard deadline (event, shoot, competition) who want unlimited access and complete outsourcing of training and nutrition decisions.
- **Pricing logic:** Anchored against high-end private coaching ($1,500–3,000+/mo) and concierge-medicine framing; the always-on access is the premium. **+$C/month**

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** Self-Drive (+$A/mo) — Template + 12-week + Async + Monthly + Framework nutrition + Self-directed
- **Per-dimension upgrade pricing:**
  - Depth: Template → Custom (+$D), Custom → Bespoke (+$E)
  - Format: Async → Virtual live (+$F), Virtual → Hybrid in-person (+$G)
  - Support: Monthly → Bi-weekly (+$H), Bi-weekly → Weekly (+$I), Weekly → Daily (+$J)
  - Nutrition: Framework → Tracked macros (+$K), Tracked → Meal plan (+$L)
  - Accountability: Self → Logged (+$M), Logged → Coached (+$N), Coached → Concierge (+$O)
- **Floor:** Self-Drive base — anything less isn't a coaching relationship, it's a PDF.
- **Ceiling:** If BYO config exceeds ~85% of Concierge price OR includes Daily support + Bespoke depth + In-person → auto-route to Concierge (or custom quote).
- **Configurator dependencies enforced:** see invalid combinations in Part 2.
- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO summed to reach Coached's exact configuration must price ~15–20% above Coached's flat rate. BYO summed to Concierge must price ~20–25% above Concierge flat. Flagships remain the rational choice for clients who want that full configuration.

---

Want me to **re-anchor with real $ figures** (give me your current 1-on-1 monthly rate + sub-niche), or **identify the highest-leverage atom** to standardize first so BYO doesn't drown you in ops?

*Assuming mid-market change management means org-restructure, M&A integration, or major systems rollout (ERP/CRM) for 200–2,000-employee companies; will re-anchor with $ figures once you name the dominant change type and typical engagement length.*

---

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Stakeholder map
- Change-readiness assessment report
- Communication plan (cascade + channels)
- Training curriculum outline
- Training delivery (live or async)
- Resistance/risk register
- Adoption metrics dashboard
- Executive playbook
- Post-go-live retrospective

**Time blocks**
- 60-min executive kickoff
- 45-min stakeholder interview (per stakeholder)
- 90-min leadership alignment workshop
- Half-day all-hands launch session
- Weekly 60-min steering committee call
- Bi-weekly 30-min sponsor check-in
- Office hours (2 hr/week, async-first)

**Access tiers**
- Email-only (48-hr response)
- Slack/Teams shared channel (24-hr response)
- Direct line to lead consultant (same-day)
- On-site presence (1 day/wk, 2 days/wk, embedded)

**Support levels**
- Document review (1, 3, unlimited rounds)
- Coaching for change champions (group / 1-on-1)
- Train-the-trainer certification
- Post-launch hypercare window (30 / 60 / 90 days)

**Customizations**
- Change type (restructure / M&A / systems / culture)
- # of stakeholders interviewed (5 / 12 / 25+)
- # of business units in scope
- Internal comms team capability (DIY-ready vs. needs heavy lift)
- Geography (single-site / multi-site / global)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Depth** | Diagnostic & interview thoroughness | Light (5 interviews, survey) / Standard (12 interviews, survey, focus groups) / Deep (25+ interviews, ethnographic, multi-method) | Deep blocks Sprint pace |
| **Breadth** | Org scope | Single BU / Multi-BU / Enterprise-wide | Enterprise requires Standard depth minimum |
| **Pace** | Calendar | Sprint (6 wk) / Standard (12 wk) / Extended (24 wk + hypercare) | Sprint blocks Deep depth and Embedded access |
| **Format** | Delivery mode | Remote-only / Hybrid (1 day/wk on-site) / Embedded (2+ days/wk) | Embedded requires Extended pace |
| **Support** | Hypercare & coaching | None / 30-day / 60-day / 90-day + champion coaching | 90-day requires Standard or Extended pace |
| **Add-ons** | Optional modules | Train-the-trainer · Adoption dashboard · Executive coaching · M&A culture-integration module · Multi-language rollout | M&A module requires Multi-BU or Enterprise breadth |
| **Change Type** *(domain-specific)* | Drives playbook & risk register | Restructure / M&A integration / Systems rollout / Culture | M&A requires Hybrid or Embedded format |
| **Sponsor Engagement** *(domain-specific)* | Executive intensity | Quarterly check-ins / Bi-weekly / Weekly + on-call | Weekly+ requires Hybrid or Embedded |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint pace + Deep depth
- Sprint pace + Embedded format
- Enterprise breadth + Light depth
- 90-day hypercare + Sprint pace
- M&A change type + Remote-only format
- Embedded format + Standard or Sprint pace
- Train-the-trainer add-on without at least Standard depth

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing Anchor |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Diagnostic Sprint** (Lite) | Light depth · Single BU · Sprint pace · Remote · No hypercare · No add-ons | For mid-market leaders who suspect a change is going sideways and need an outside read fast — readiness assessment + 30-day action plan. | **+$A** — anchored at ~25% of flagship; entry-point / land-and-expand |
| **Integrated Engagement** (Core, *flagship*) | Standard depth · Multi-BU · Standard pace · Hybrid · 60-day hypercare · Adoption dashboard | For companies actually executing a major change (systems rollout, restructure, post-merger 90-day) and need a partner from kickoff through adoption. | **+$B** — anchored below Big-4 advisory equivalents; senior-led, no junior staffing pyramid |
| **Embedded Transformation** (Premium) | Deep depth · Enterprise breadth · Extended pace · Embedded · 90-day hypercare + champion coaching · Train-the-trainer + executive coaching | For enterprise-scale change (M&A integration, multi-region systems rollout) where the cost of failure dwarfs the engagement fee. | **+$C** — priced below sum of parts (~15–20% bundle discount) and below boutique consultancy retainers for equivalent scope |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** Diagnostic Sprint (+$A)
- **Per-dimension upgrades:**
  - Depth: Light → Standard (+$D1), Standard → Deep (+$D2)
  - Breadth: Single → Multi-BU (+$B1), Multi → Enterprise (+$B2)
  - Pace: Sprint → Standard (+$P1), Standard → Extended (+$P2)
  - Format: Remote → Hybrid (+$F1), Hybrid → Embedded (+$F2)
  - Support: per 30-day hypercare block (+$S each)
  - Add-ons: each priced à la carte (+$AO1...n)
- **Floor:** +$A (Diagnostic Sprint config) — below this, refer to a 2-hour paid advisory call instead
- **Ceiling:** above ~$C × 1.1 → auto-route to Premium or scoped custom proposal
- **Configurator dependencies:** enforce all blocks in Part 2 (Sprint+Deep, Sprint+Embedded, Enterprise+Light, etc.)
- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO-summing to the Core configuration must price ~12–15% **above** +$B; BYO-summing to Premium config must price ~15–20% **above** +$C. Flagships are the rational discount; à la carte is the premium for optionality.

---

**Want me to:** (a) re-anchor with real $ figures once you name the dominant change type and typical deal size, (b) emit Parts 2–3 as JSON for a configurator/Typeform, or (c) stress-test against a specific scenario (e.g., "800-person company, ERP rollout, 9 months")?

This skill doesn't apply to your offering.

**Why:** Intelligent Clay productizes *services* — consulting, coaching, agency, freelance, done-for-you work — by decomposing labor into configurable atoms. You're already running a SaaS product with three tiers and seat-based pricing. Software is inherently modular; the structural problem this skill solves doesn't exist in your case.

**What you likely actually want** (none of which are this skill):

- **Packaging & pricing strategy** — feature-to-tier mapping, value metric selection (seats vs. usage vs. outcome), Enterprise gating logic. Look for a SaaS pricing/packaging framework (e.g., Kyle Poyar / OpenView style).
- **Product roadmap / discovery** — what to build next. Use a PM-oriented skill or framework (RICE, opportunity solution trees, JTBD).
- **Positioning** — if Pro vs. Enterprise vs. freemium isn't landing with the right buyers. Use an April Dunford-style positioning skill.
- **Expansion revenue / PLG motion design** — freemium→Pro conversion, Pro→Enterprise expansion triggers. Different discipline entirely.

**The one edge case where I'd be useful:** if your Enterprise plan includes a meaningful *services* component — onboarding, implementation, custom integrations, dedicated CSM hours, training — and you want to productize *that services layer* into Lite/Core/Premium implementation packages plus a build-your-own configurator. That's a clay problem.

If that's the case, tell me what's inside Enterprise beyond the software itself and I'll run the skill on the services layer.

This is out of scope for this skill.

**Why:** Intelligent Clay productizes *services* — consulting, coaching, agencies, done-for-you work — by decomposing labor and access into configurable atoms. You're selling a *physical product* with a fixed unit cost structure (leather, hardware, your time per wallet). That's a different problem.

**What you actually want is SKU/bundle/configurator logic for physical goods**, which looks like:

- **Variants** — leather type, color, thread color, hardware finish
- **Configurations** — bifold / cardholder / long wallet / money clip
- **Personalization** — monogram, custom stamping, gift packaging
- **Bundles** — wallet + belt, wallet + keychain, his/hers sets
- **Tiers by materials**, not by labor — standard leather ($120) / premium leather ($180) / exotic ($300+)

That's a product-line-architecture question, not a service-productization one. Shopify's variant system, a made-to-order configurator (e.g., Kickflip, Zakeke), or a "build your wallet" flow handles this natively.

**If your situation is actually different — for example:**

- You also offer **custom commissions** (design consult → bespoke wallet) — *that* portion is a service and I can modularize it.
- You run **leatherworking workshops or coaching** — that's a service.
- You do **wholesale/B2B custom runs for brands** (logo embossing, corporate gifts at volume) — that's a productizable service.

…tell me which and I'll run the skill on the service layer. Otherwise, you want a product-merchandising resource, not this one.

*Assuming this is a hybrid offering: the $99/mo SaaS subscription is one fixed atom (not decomposable here), and the $2,500 1-week implementation service is the clay. I'll productize the service portion only; will re-anchor pricing if you share what specific enterprise vertical you sell into.*

---

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Configured production tenant (workspace, roles, permissions, SSO)
- Data migration: source-to-target field mapping doc
- Migrated historical data (one source system)
- Integration setup (one third-party connector: e.g., Slack, Salesforce, HubSpot)
- Custom workflow/automation configuration
- Admin runbook (PDF, customer-specific)
- Recorded training videos (customer-specific)
- 30-60-90 success plan document

**Time blocks**
- 60-min kickoff + scoping call
- 45-min technical discovery call (data + integration audit)
- 90-min admin training session
- 60-min end-user training session (per cohort)
- 30-min go-live checkpoint
- 30-min 2-week post-launch review

**Access tiers**
- Async-only (shared Slack channel, 1 business day SLA)
- Dedicated implementation manager (named contact, 4-hour SLA during implementation week)
- Direct line to engineering (for custom data/integration work)

**Support levels**
- Post-launch office hours (1x, 2x, or 4x 30-min sessions in first 30 days)
- Revision rounds on workflow config (1, 2, unlimited within scope)
- Migration retry/cleanup pass

**Customizations (variables)**
- # of users to onboard (≤25 / 26–100 / 100+)
- # of source systems to migrate from (1 / 2 / 3+)
- # of integrations to configure (0 / 1 / 2+)
- Training cohorts (1 / 2–3 / 4+ separate sessions)
- Timeline (1-week sprint / 2-week standard / 4-week phased rollout)
- Custom workflow complexity (templates only / light customization / deep customization)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Depth** | Workflow customization & migration cleanup | Templates-only / Light-custom / Deep-custom | Deep-custom blocked at Sprint pace |
| **Breadth** | Scope of users, sources, integrations | Single-team / Multi-team / Org-wide | Org-wide requires Standard or Phased pace |
| **Pace** | Implementation timeline | Sprint (1w) / Standard (2w) / Phased (4w) | Sprint blocks Deep-custom and 3+ source migrations |
| **Migration Scope** *(domain-specific)* | # of source systems + historical data volume | None / 1 source / 2+ sources | 2+ sources requires Standard or Phased; engineering access required |
| **Training Format** | How end-users get trained | Recorded-only / Live-1-cohort / Live-multi-cohort | Live-multi-cohort requires Multi-team or Org-wide breadth |
| **Support** | Access + post-launch hand-holding | Async / Dedicated IM / IM + Eng line | IM + Eng line requires 2+ sources OR Deep-custom |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Sprint pace + Deep-custom workflows
- Sprint pace + 2+ source migration
- Org-wide breadth + Sprint pace
- Live-multi-cohort training + Single-team breadth
- IM + Eng line on a Templates-only / 0-integration / 0-migration config (over-provisioned)
- 4+ training cohorts + Recorded-only (contradiction)

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Price |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Launch Sprint** | Templates-only · Single-team · Sprint (1w) · No migration · Recorded training · Async support | For small enterprise teams (≤25 users) with no legacy data who need to be live next week | **$1,500** |
| **Core — Standard Onboarding** *(flagship, current offer reframed)* | Light-custom · Multi-team · Standard (2w) · 1 source migration · Live 1-cohort training · Dedicated IM | For typical new enterprise customers: one team to migrate, one cohort to train, live in two weeks | **$2,500** |
| **Premium — Org Rollout** | Deep-custom · Org-wide · Phased (4w) · 2+ source migration · Live multi-cohort training · IM + Eng line | For 100+ user rollouts with multiple legacy systems and custom workflows; phased go-live by department | **$7,500** |

**Pricing logic / anchors:**
- **Lite ($1,500):** ~60% of flagship; floor that still covers a kickoff + setup + recorded handoff. Anchored against the implicit cost of self-serve failure (churn risk in month 1).
- **Core ($2,500):** Your existing flagship, preserved. Stays the rational default — BYO sum to reach this config must price above $2,500 (see floor rule below).
- **Premium ($7,500):** 3x flagship. Anchored against the alternative — hiring a Solutions Architect contractor at $150–200/hr for ~40 hours = $6–8K, with no product expertise. Premium undercuts that and includes engineering access.

### Build-Your-Own framework

**Base price:** $1,500 (Lite configuration)

**Per-dimension upgrades:**
- Depth: Light-custom +$500 · Deep-custom +$2,000
- Breadth: Multi-team +$400 · Org-wide +$1,500
- Pace: Sprint $0 · Standard +$300 · Phased +$800
- Migration: 1 source +$600 · 2+ sources +$1,800
- Training: Live-1-cohort +$400 · Live-multi-cohort +$1,200
- Support: Dedicated IM +$500 · IM + Eng line +$1,500

**Floor:** $1,500 (Lite as-is — anything below would be unprofitable handoff)

**Ceiling:** $7,000 — above this, auto-route to Premium flagship ($7,500) since it's now cheaper than the BYO sum.

**Configurator-enforced dependencies:** all invalid combinations from Part 2, plus auto-suggest IM + Eng line when migration ≥ 2 sources is selected.

**Flagship-protection check:**
- BYO to reach Core's config: $1,500 + $500 (Light-custom) + $400 (Multi-team) + $300 (Standard pace) + $600 (1 source) + $400 (Live-1-cohort) + $500 (Dedicated IM) = **$4,200** vs. Core flagship **$2,500** ✅ Core wins by $1,700.
- BYO to reach Premium's config: $1,500 + $2,000 + $1,500 + $800 + $1,800 + $1,200 + $1,500 = **$10,300** vs. Premium flagship **$7,500** ✅ Premium wins by $2,800.

Both flagships remain the rational discount.

---

**Want me to follow up with one of these?**
- Re-emit Parts 2 and 3 as JSON/YAML for a configurator (Typeform, Webflow, or custom)
- Identify the 1–2 highest-leverage atoms to standardize first (likely migration templates and recorded training) to unlock BYO velocity
- Stress-test the dimensions against a specific edge-case customer you've recently onboarded

*Assuming general-professional resume writing (mid-career knowledge workers, $80K–$200K range) with you doing the writing yourself; will re-anchor if you serve a specific vertical (tech, exec, federal, academic, career-changers).*

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Resume rewrite (1 page or 2 page)
- ATS-optimized version (keyword-tuned to a target role/JD)
- Plain-text / .txt version for online forms
- LinkedIn "About" + headline rewrite
- LinkedIn full profile rewrite (experience bullets, skills, featured)
- Cover letter template (role-agnostic)
- Cover letter (tailored to one specific job)
- Achievement-mining worksheet (client-completed pre-work)
- Bullet bank (extra accomplishments not used in final resume, for future tailoring)
- Interview-ready talking points doc (top 5 stories matched to resume bullets)

**Time blocks**
- 20-min intake call
- 45-min discovery interview (resume-only)
- 75-min deep career interview (resume + LinkedIn + positioning)
- 30-min walkthrough call (delivering the draft, talking through choices)

**Access tiers**
- Email-only, 48-hr response
- Email, 24-hr response
- Email + text, same-day response
- Slack/Voxer async during engagement window

**Support levels**
- Zero revisions (locked on delivery)
- 1 revision round within 7 days
- 2 revision rounds within 14 days
- 30-day "tweak window" — unlimited small edits
- 90-day re-tailor — one fresh tailoring to a new job posting

**Customizations**
- Length (1pg vs 2pg)
- Targeting (general / one role-type / one specific JD)
- Industry focus (generalist vs specialized vocabulary)
- Turnaround (10 business days / 5 / 3 / 48hr rush)
- Format depth (Word only / Word + PDF / Word + PDF + .txt + LinkedIn-paste version)

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Depth | Interview + discovery intensity | Async-form-only / 45-min call / 75-min deep | Deep required for Premium positioning work |
| Breadth | What gets written | Resume only / Resume + LinkedIn / Resume + LinkedIn + Cover Letter / Full career kit | LinkedIn rewrite requires at least 45-min call |
| Targeting | How tailored the output is | Generalist / Role-type-targeted / Specific-JD-targeted | Specific-JD requires client to provide JD at intake |
| Pace | Turnaround | Standard 10bd / Priority 5bd / Rush 3bd / Emergency 48hr | Rush + Deep blocked; Emergency available only on Resume-only scope |
| Support | Revisions + access | None / 1 round / 2 rounds + email / Unlimited 30d + Slack | Unlimited support requires Priority pace or slower |
| Format | Output artifacts | Word / Word+PDF / Full pack (Word+PDF+.txt+LinkedIn-paste) | Full pack requires Breadth ≥ Resume+LinkedIn |
| Re-tailor window | Future-proofing | None / 90-day re-tailor / 12-mo retainer (quarterly tailor) | Retainer requires Core or Premium starting tier |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Rush 3bd + Deep interview (not enough calendar)
- Emergency 48hr + any breadth beyond Resume-only
- LinkedIn rewrite without at least one live call
- Full pack format on Resume-only breadth
- Specific-JD targeting without JD uploaded at intake
- Unlimited support window on Rush or Emergency pace
- 12-mo retainer on Lite tier

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Price |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — The Rewrite** | Async intake form, Resume-only, Generalist or Role-type targeting, Standard 10bd, 1 revision round, Word+PDF, no re-tailor | For job-seekers who know what they want and just need a sharper document | **$400** (your current price — kept as the entry anchor) |
| **Core — The Job-Search Kit** *(flagship)* | 45-min interview, Resume + LinkedIn + Cover Letter template, Specific-JD targeting, Priority 5bd, 2 revisions + email support, Full pack, 90-day re-tailor | For active job-seekers running a real search who need consistent voice across resume, LinkedIn, and outreach | **$950** (vs. $400 + ~$350 LinkedIn + ~$150 cover letter + rush ≈ $1,100 à la carte; flagship saves ~$150) |
| **Premium — The Career Narrative** | 75-min deep interview, Full career kit (Resume + LinkedIn + tailored Cover Letter + bullet bank + interview talking points), Specific-JD targeting, Priority 5bd, Unlimited 30-day tweaks + Slack, Full pack, 12-mo quarterly re-tailor retainer | For career-changers, senior ICs moving to leadership, or anyone whose story needs reframing — not just rewriting | **$2,400** (vs. ~$2,800+ summed à la carte; positions you as career strategist, not resume typist) |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

**Base:** $400 (Lite config)

**Per-dimension upgrades:**
- Depth: +$150 for 45-min call · +$350 for 75-min deep interview
- Breadth: +$300 LinkedIn · +$150 cover letter template · +$250 tailored cover letter · +$200 bullet bank · +$300 interview talking points
- Targeting: +$100 role-type · +$200 specific-JD
- Pace: +$150 Priority 5bd · +$300 Rush 3bd · +$500 Emergency 48hr
- Support: +$100 second revision round · +$400 unlimited 30-day + Slack
- Re-tailor: +$200 90-day re-tailor · +$1,200 12-mo quarterly retainer

**Floor:** $400 (Lite config — no lower configuration sold)
**Ceiling:** Any BYO totaling >$2,200 auto-routes to Premium consultation (you don't want to deliver Premium-scope work without the deep interview)

**Configurator dependencies (enforced):**
- Selecting LinkedIn → forces Depth ≥ 45-min call
- Selecting specific-JD targeting → requires JD upload field
- Selecting Rush/Emergency → hides Deep interview option
- Selecting unlimited support → hides Rush/Emergency pace
- Selecting retainer → requires base tier ≥ Core-equivalent BYO ($900+)

**Flagship-protection rule:**
- BYO to match Core config: $400 + $150 + $300 + $150 + $200 + $150 + $100 + $200 = **$1,650** vs. flagship **$950** ✓
- BYO to match Premium config: $400 + $350 + $300 + $250 + $200 + $300 + $200 + $150 + $400 + $1,200 = **$3,750** vs. flagship **$2,400** ✓

Flagships remain the rational discount; BYO punishes cherry-picking.

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Intake assessment / baseline diagnostic
- Goal-setting document (90-day or engagement-length)
- Session recap notes (per session)
- Personalized action plan / playbook
- Mid-engagement progress review
- Final outcome report / next-chapter roadmap
- Resource library access (frameworks, templates, reading)

**Time blocks**
- 90-min intake / kickoff session
- 60-min weekly 1-on-1 coaching call
- 45-min bi-weekly coaching call
- 30-min laser / spot-coaching call
- 60-min mid-engagement review
- 90-min quarterly deep-dive

**Access tiers**
- Email-only between sessions (48hr response)
- Async messaging (Voxer/Slack/WhatsApp, 24hr response)
- Same-day async response window
- Emergency / SOS call privilege (1–2 per engagement)

**Support levels**
- Session recap notes from coach
- Action-item tracking between sessions
- Pre-session prep prompts

**Customizations**

- Stakeholder involvement (manager, partner, board)

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Duration** | Engagement length | 3mo / 6mo / 12mo | 12mo requires quarterly deep-dives |
| **Cadence** | Session frequency | Bi-weekly / Weekly / Weekly + laser | On-demand requires async-same-day access |
| **Depth** | Diagnostic & assessment rigor | Self-assessment / Coach-led intake / Full 360 + instruments | Full 360 requires min 6mo duration |
| **Access** | Between-session contact | Email 48hr / Async 24hr / Same-day + SOS | SOS only at 6mo+ duration |
| **Support** | Structured artifacts | Recaps only / Recaps + action tracking / Full playbook + prep prompts | Playbook requires Coach-led intake or higher |
| **Focus Mix** *(domain-specific)* | Topical specialization | Single track / Dual track / Open-agenda | Open-agenda requires Weekly cadence |
| **Stakeholder Loop** *(domain-specific)* | External voices in the engagement | None / Manager check-ins / Full 360 interviews | Requires Full 360 depth |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Bi-weekly cadence + Same-day access (mismatched intensity)
- 3mo duration + Full 360 depth (insufficient runway)
- 3mo duration + SOS access
- Open-agenda focus + Bi-weekly cadence (loses momentum)
- Stakeholder Loop without Full 360 depth
- Full playbook support + Self-assessment depth (no input to build from)

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing Logic |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Focused Sprint** | 3mo · Bi-weekly · Coach-led intake · Email 48hr · Recaps + action tracking · Single track · No stakeholders | For someone with one clear goal who needs accountability and structure, not transformation. | **+$A** — entry anchor; ~35–40% of flagship; priced to beat "I'll just hire a coach for a few sessions" hourly math |
| **Core — Signature Engagement** *(flagship)* | 6mo · Weekly · Coach-led intake · Async 24hr · Recaps + action tracking · Dual track · Manager check-ins | For professionals navigating a meaningful transition or stretch — the default engagement most clients should buy. | **+$B** — flagship; priced below sum-of-parts BYO equivalent; anchored against typical 6mo coaching engagements in your niche |
| **Premium — Embedded Partnership** | 12mo · Weekly + laser · Full 360 + instruments · Same-day + SOS · Full playbook + prep prompts · Open-agenda · Full 360 interviews | For senior leaders or founders who need a thinking partner embedded across the year, not a series of sessions. | **+$C** — priced below 12× monthly retainer of comparable senior coaches; anchored against fractional-advisor pricing |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** Lite configuration at **+$A**
- **Per-dimension upgrade pricing** (illustrative tokens):
  - Duration: 3mo→6mo +$D · 6mo→12mo +$E
  - Cadence: Bi-weekly→Weekly +$F · Weekly→Weekly+laser +$G
  - Depth: Coach-led→Full 360 +$H
  - Access: Email→Async +$I · Async→Same-day+SOS +$J
  - Support: Recaps→Full playbook +$K
  - Stakeholder Loop: +$L
- **Floor:** Lite base (+$A); below this, refer to single-session or group cohort
- **Ceiling:** If BYO config sums above Premium price, auto-route to Premium or custom proposal
- **Configurator must enforce:** all invalid combinations from Part 2; auto-upgrade Depth when Stakeholder Loop is selected; auto-extend Duration when SOS or Full 360 is selected
- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO-summing the Core configuration (6mo + Weekly + Async + Dual track + Manager check-ins) must price **above** Core's flagship price by ~15–20%. Same for Premium. Flagships are the rational discount.

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tracking Scope | Keywords + competitors + SERP features monitored | 10kw / 30kw / 75kw / Unlimited; +0/2/5 competitors | Unlimited requires Pro-tier reporting cadence |
| Content Velocity | Articles or briefs delivered/mo | 0 / 2 briefs / 2 articles / 4 articles / 8 articles | 4+ articles requires weekly call (coordination overhead) |
| Reporting Cadence | Frequency + format of performance reporting | Monthly written / Biweekly + call / Weekly + call | Weekly cadence requires Slack access |
| Access | Communication channel + SLA | Email 48hr / Slack 24hr / Dedicated 4hr | Dedicated requires Biweekly+ cadence |
| Link Building | Off-page acquisition intensity | None / 2/mo DR30+ / 5/mo DR50+ | 5/mo requires named strategist |
| Technical Depth | Audit and tech-SEO involvement | None / Quarterly audit / Continuous + dev handoff | Continuous requires weekly call |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block**
- Unlimited tracking + Monthly cadence (volume can't be interpreted in one call/mo)
- 4+ articles/mo without weekly call
- Dedicated Slack access without at least Biweekly cadence
- 5/mo link building without named strategist
- Continuous technical SEO without weekly call
- BYO total below $2,000/mo floor (auto-route to Starter)
- BYO total above $10,500/mo (auto-route to Pro or custom)

## Part 3 — Existing Tiers Preserved + BYO Overlay

**Existing tiers stay exactly as-is:**

| Tier | Price | Configuration |
|---|---|---|
| Starter | $2,000/mo | 10kw, Monthly report, Email |
| Growth (flagship) | $5,000/mo | 30kw, Biweekly + call, 1 content brief, Slack 24hr |
| Pro | $10,000/mo | Unlimited kw, Weekly + call, 4 articles, Dedicated Slack |

**BYO slots underneath Starter and between tiers:**

- **BYO floor:** $2,000/mo — at or below this, route to Starter
- **BYO ceiling:** $10,500/mo — above this, route to Pro or custom scope
- **BYO sweet spot:** $2,500–$9,500/mo — between-tier custom builds

**Base + per-dimension upgrade pricing**

Base config (matches Starter floor): **$2,000/mo** = 10kw + Monthly + Email

| Upgrade | Add |
|---|---|
| Tracking: 30kw | +$800 |
| Tracking: 75kw | +$2,000 |
| Tracking: Unlimited | +$3,500 |
| +2 competitors tracked | +$400 |
| +5 competitors tracked | +$900 |
| Cadence: Biweekly + call | +$1,200 |
| Cadence: Weekly + call | +$2,800 |
| Access: Slack 24hr | +$500 |
| Access: Dedicated 4hr | +$1,500 |
| Content: 2 briefs/mo | +$1,000 |
| Content: 2 articles/mo | +$2,200 |
| Content: 4 articles/mo | +$4,000 |
| Content: 8 articles/mo | +$7,500 |
| Links: 2/mo DR30+ | +$1,500 |
| Links: 5/mo DR50+ | +$4,500 |
| Tech: Quarterly audit | +$600 |
| Tech: Continuous + handoff | +$2,200 |

**Flagship-protection check (BYO sum vs. existing tier price)**

| Tier | If built à la carte via BYO | Flagship price | Discount |
|---|---|---|---|
| Starter (10kw, monthly, email) | $2,000 | $2,000 | tie (BYO floor = Starter) |
| Growth (30kw, biweekly+call, 1 brief, Slack) | $2,000 + $800 + $1,200 + $500 + $500 (half-brief proxy)¹ = **$5,000+** | $5,000 | tie/marginal — **adjust:** make Growth include 1 brief at value $1,000, BYO equivalent = **$5,500**, Growth wins by $500 |
| Pro (unlimited kw, weekly+call, 4 articles, dedicated) | $2,000 + $3,500 + $2,800 + $1,500 + $4,000 = **$13,800** | $10,000 | Pro wins by $3,800 ✓ |

¹ Recommend: don't sell half-briefs in BYO. Minimum content unit in BYO = 2 briefs/mo ($1,000) or 2 articles/mo ($2,200). This forces Growth's 1-brief-included to remain a flagship perk.

**Configurator dependencies (enforced rules)**
- If Content Velocity ≥ 4 articles → Cadence auto-locks to Weekly
- If Access = Dedicated → Cadence must be ≥ Biweekly
- If Link Building = 5/mo → named strategist toggle on (+included, no extra fee, but locks pricing tier)
- If Tracking = Unlimited → Cadence must be Weekly
- If BYO total < $2,000 → "You'd be better served by Starter at $2,000" CTA
- If BYO total > $10,500 → "Let's talk — Pro at $10,000 likely covers this, or we'll quote custom"

**Flagship-protection rule (codified):** Any BYO configuration that *matches or exceeds* a named tier's spec must price ≥ $500 above that tier. Configurator should display: "You're $X away from [Tier Name] which includes everything you've selected — switch?" whenever within $750 of a tier price with comparable spec.

This skill produces structural blueprints for productizing services — atomic decomposition, configuration architecture, and tiered packaging. It doesn't produce offer copy, guarantees, or bonus stacks. That's positioning/marketing work, which is out of scope.

Two paths forward:

**If you want offer copy** — that's a different skill (Hormozi-style offer construction, value-equation work, guarantee design). I'd point you to an offer-extraction or direct-response copy skill instead.

**If you want to productize the $5K sales page service** — I can do that. It would give you:
- A Lite/Core/Premium tier structure (e.g., $2K landing-page sprint / $5K full sales page / $12K sales page + funnel + revisions retainer)
- A Build-Your-Own configurator so prospects who can't afford $5K still convert at a lower tier, and bigger prospects can stack upgrades past $5K
- Dimensions like Depth (research intensity), Breadth (sales page only vs. + emails + VSL script), Pace (rush vs. standard), Support (revision rounds, post-launch optimization)

Productized structure tends to outperform a single-price offer with bonuses bolted on, because it captures buyers above *and* below your current price point — and the tiers themselves create the urgency and anchoring that bonus stacks try to manufacture.

# Productization Blueprint: Enterprise Client Course

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Curriculum library (recorded video lessons, structured by module)
- Templates pack (cold outreach, proposal, SOW, MSA redlines, pricing sheet)
- Swipe file (real enterprise emails, RFP responses, contract excerpts)
- Frameworks (ICP scoring, deal-stage tracker, account-mapping worksheet)
- Final 1-on-1 review write-up (personalized feedback document)

**Time blocks**
- 60-min weekly live session (8 total over the cohort)
- 60-min final 1-on-1 review call
- Optional: live office hours (group Q&A, weekly)
- Optional: hot-seat slot (15-min spotlight in a live session)

**Access tiers**
- Slack community access (peer + alumni)
- Instructor Slack access (DM, async, response SLA)
- Cohort cohort-mate access (time-bound, your specific cohort)

**Support levels**
- Async template/proposal review (turnaround tier: 5-day / 2-day)
- Live cohort participation (synchronous, scheduled)
- Self-paced (no live component)
- Pitch-deck or proposal redline (1 artifact reviewed)

**Customizations**
- Cohort vs. self-paced
- Industry focus (SaaS / agencies / specialized verticals)
- Service type (design / dev / strategy / writing)
- Deal-size target ($25K / $100K / $500K+)
- Solo freelancer vs. small studio (1–5 people)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Pace** | Calendar structure | Self-paced / Cohort (8 weeks) / Cohort + extended alumni | 1-on-1 review requires Cohort or higher |
| **Depth** | Personalization of feedback | None / Group review / 1-on-1 review / Multi-touch coaching | 1-on-1 requires submitted artifact |
| **Access** | Who you can reach + how fast | Community Slack only / +Instructor async (5d) / +Instructor priority (2d) | Priority requires Cohort+ |
| **Breadth** | Curriculum scope | Outreach-only / Outreach+Proposal / Full pipeline (outreach→close→expand) | — |
| **Format** | Sync vs. async live | Recordings only / Live sessions / Live + hot-seat slot | Hot-seat requires Live |
| **Targeting** *(domain-specific)* | Deal-size / vertical fit | Generic / Vertical-tagged content / Vertical-matched cohort | Vertical-matched cohort runs only when ≥6 enroll |
| **Add-ons** | Discrete artifacts | Proposal redline / Deck redline / Account-map session / Mock pitch | Mock pitch requires Live |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Self-paced + 1-on-1 review (no instructor relationship to anchor it — route to Core)
- Self-paced + Priority instructor access
- Hot-seat slot without Live sessions
- Mock pitch without at least one live session attended
- Vertical-matched cohort below 6-person enrollment threshold (auto-fall-back to vertical-tagged)

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

| Tier | Configuration | Positioning | Pricing |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lite — Self-Paced Library** | Pace: Self-paced · Depth: None · Access: Community only · Breadth: Full pipeline · Format: Recordings | For freelancers who want the playbook + templates without the calendar commitment | **$400** — anchored at ~33% of flagship; entry that pays back on a single $5K enterprise deal |
| **Core — Cohort *(flagship)*** | Pace: Cohort 8wk · Depth: 1-on-1 review · Access: Instructor async (5d) · Breadth: Full · Format: Live | For freelancers actively pitching enterprise this quarter who want accountability + a personal review | **$1,200** — current price; anchored against the income lift of one mid-five-figure deal |
| **Premium — Cohort + Coaching** | Pace: Cohort + extended alumni · Depth: Multi-touch coaching · Access: Priority (2d) · Breadth: Full · Format: Live + hot-seat · Add-ons: Proposal + Deck redline + Mock pitch | For studios or freelancers chasing a specific named enterprise account during the cohort | **$2,800** — below the sum-of-parts (BYO equivalent ≈ $3,400); priced as the "we're going to land this account together" tier |

### Build-Your-Own Framework

**Base:** $400 (Self-Paced Library — full curriculum, templates, community Slack)

**Per-dimension upgrades:**
- Add Cohort live sessions (8wk): **+$500**
- Add 1-on-1 final review: **+$300**
- Add instructor async Slack (5d SLA): **+$200**
- Upgrade async → priority (2d SLA): **+$200**
- Add hot-seat slot: **+$150**
- Add proposal redline: **+$250**
- Add deck redline: **+$250**
- Add account-map working session (60 min): **+$350**
- Add mock pitch session: **+$400**
- Add multi-touch coaching (3 additional 1-on-1s): **+$700**
- Extended alumni access (12mo): **+$200**

**Floor:** $400 (Lite — anything below loses the curriculum, which is the load-bearing IP)

**Ceiling:** $2,400 — above this, auto-route to Premium (cheaper) or to a custom 1-on-1 coaching engagement

**Configurator-enforced dependencies:** the five blocked combinations from Part 2.

**Flagship-protection check:**
- BYO to reach Core's config: $400 + $500 + $300 + $200 = **$1,400** (Core flagship $1,200 ✓ wins by $200)
- BYO to reach Premium's config: $400 + $500 + $700 + $200 + $200 + $150 + $250 + $250 + $400 + $200 = **$3,250** (Premium flagship $2,800 ✓ wins by $450)

Both flagships remain the rational discount.

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables**
- Brand/campaign strategy brief
- Visual identity asset (logo system, type, color)
- Design system / component library
- Landing page or microsite design
- Long-form copy (web, manifesto, narrative)
- Short-form copy (ads, social, email)
- Motion asset — logo animation / brand bumper (≤15s)
- Motion asset — explainer / hero video (30–90s)
- Motion asset — campaign cutdowns (social-native)
- Static campaign creative (digital ads, OOH layouts)
- Quarterly creative roadmap
- Monthly creative-performance review deck

**Time blocks**
- 60-min kickoff
- 90-min strategy workshop
- 45-min stakeholder interview
- 30-min weekly creative review
- 60-min monthly executive readout
- 2-week design sprint
- 4-week campaign sprint

**Access tiers**
- Slack Connect, named lead, <4hr business-hours response
- Slack Connect, shared queue, <24hr response
- Email-only, <48hr response
- Monthly check-in only, async

**Support levels**
- Unlimited revisions within sprint scope
- 2 revision rounds per deliverable
- 1 revision round per deliverable
- Async creative review (Loom-based, no live calls)
- Embedded creative-director hours (4/8/16 hrs/mo)

**Customizations**
- Discipline mix (strategy/design/copy/motion ratio)
- Output cadence (per-month deliverable count by discipline)
- Industry vertical specialization
- Brand-stage fit (pre-launch, scale-up, rebrand, mature)
- Channel focus (brand vs. performance vs. product)
- Rights & usage scope (campaign-bound vs. perpetual)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Discipline Mix** | Which of strategy/design/copy/motion are active and at what weight | Single-discipline / Dual / Tri / Full quad | Motion at "primary" requires Design active; Strategy-only blocks all production deliverables |
| **Depth** | Senior-talent involvement, research rigor | Production-led / Senior-led / Partner-embedded | Partner-embedded blocked below Tri-discipline |
| **Breadth (Output Volume)** | Deliverables per month | Light (2–4) / Standard (5–9) / Heavy (10–16) | Heavy blocked at Production-led depth |
| **Pace** | Sprint cadence | Monthly drop / Bi-weekly sprints / Continuous flow | Continuous flow requires Standard or Heavy breadth |
| **Access** | Channel + response time | Async only / Shared Slack / Named lead Slack | Named lead requires Senior-led or Partner-embedded |
| **Support** | Revisions + creative-director hours | 1 round / 2 rounds / Unlimited in-sprint + CD hours | Unlimited requires Bi-weekly or Continuous pace |
| **Rights** | Usage scope | Campaign-bound / 12-month / Perpetual + source files | — |
| **Add-ons** | Discrete extras | Naming · Brand refresh · Motion package · Pitch-deck sprint · Embedded CD hours | Motion package requires Design active |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Production-led depth + Heavy breadth (quality collapse)
- Continuous flow + Light breadth (pace mismatch)
- Named-lead Slack + Production-led depth (no senior to name)
- Partner-embedded + Single-discipline (overpriced for scope)
- Perpetual rights + Campaign-bound pricing tier (route to custom)
- Motion package without Design active
- Unlimited revisions + Monthly drop pace (revision cycle exceeds sprint)

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

Renamed in agency vocabulary: **Studio Lite / Studio Core / Studio Atelier**.

### Studio Lite — $18K/mo

- **Configuration:** Dual-discipline (pick 2 of 4) · Senior-led · Light breadth (3–4 deliverables) · Bi-weekly sprints · Shared Slack · 2 revision rounds · 12-month rights
- **Positioning line:** For scale-ups who need senior creative output in two disciplines without a full agency partnership.
- **Pricing logic:** Anchored just above your current floor ($15K) — proves the productized version is *not* a discount; rationalizes scope, not price. Compares favorably to a senior freelancer + PM ($20–25K loaded).

### Studio Core — $42K/mo *(flagship)*

- **Configuration:** Tri-discipline (3 of 4) · Senior-led · Standard breadth (6–8 deliverables) · Bi-weekly sprints · Named-lead Slack · Unlimited in-sprint revisions · 8 CD hours/mo · 12-month rights
- **Positioning line:** For brands running integrated campaigns that need strategy, design, and one production discipline working in lockstep.
- **Pricing logic:** Sits at the median of your current $15–80K range. BYO-summed equivalent prices at ~$48–52K — flagship reads as the rational choice. Anchored against the cost of a fractional CMO ($20K) + a separate production shop ($30K+).

### Studio Atelier — $72K/mo

- **Configuration:** Full quad-discipline · Partner-embedded · Heavy breadth (10–14 deliverables) · Continuous flow · Named-lead Slack · Unlimited revisions · 16 CD hours/mo · Perpetual rights + source files
- **Positioning line:** For category leaders treating creative as a competitive moat — a full integrated studio without hiring one.
- **Pricing logic:** Below your $80K ceiling, leaving headroom for true-custom above. Anchored against a 4-person in-house creative team (~$90–110K loaded monthly). Partner-embedded depth is the unscalable atom that protects high-end positioning.

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** $18K (Studio Lite configuration)
- **Per-dimension upgrades:**
  - +Discipline (each beyond 2): +$8K
  - Depth: Senior-led included → Partner-embedded +$14K
  - Breadth: Light → Standard +$9K → Heavy +$11K (additive)
  - Pace: Bi-weekly included → Continuous +$6K
  - Access: Shared Slack included → Named lead +$3K
  - Support: 2 rounds included → Unlimited +$5K; +CD hours at $750/hr
  - Rights: 12-month included → Perpetual +$7K
- **Floor:** $18K (Studio Lite minimum) — below this, route to project work, not retainer
- **Ceiling:** $78K — above this, auto-route to **custom partnership** (preserves your $80K+ true-bespoke tier)
- **Configurator must enforce:** all invalid combinations from Part 2; rights upgrade requires breadth ≥ Standard
- **Flagship-protection rule:** BYO sum to reach Studio Core's config = ~$50K (vs. $42K flagship). BYO sum to reach Atelier = ~$80K (vs. $72K). Both flagships price 15–20% under sum-of-parts — the discount is real but modest, preserving premium feel.

---

**High-end positioning is protected by three structural choices:**
1. The Lite floor ($18K) is *above* your current floor — productizing did not cheapen entry
2. Partner-embedded depth is gated behind Tri+ discipline — the unscalable senior atom stays scarce
3. The $78K ceiling routes to custom — productization tops out *below* your true-bespoke work, not at it

## Part 1 — Atomic Decomposition

**Deliverables (artifacts)**
- Core SKILL.md file (the productization engine itself)
- Reference docs pack (`dimensions.md`, `existing-tier-overlay.md`, `configurator-output.md`, `memory-integration.md`)
- Worked examples library (per-vertical: agency, coaching, consulting, freelance, DFY)
- Self-iteration GitHub Action
- claude-mem integration adapter
- Configurator JSON/YAML schema export
- Domain-specific dimension packs (e.g., "Agency Pack," "Coaching Pack," "Resume-Writer Pack")
- Anti-pattern test suite (regression cases the skill must still pass)
- Tier-design cookbook (PDF/MDX) — codified heuristics beyond what's in the skill
- White-label / unbranded version (skill stripped of "Intelligent Clay" naming)
- Certification badge / verified-practitioner mark

**Time blocks**
- 30-min skill walkthrough (live)
- 60-min productization working session (live, applied to buyer's offering)
- 90-min team workshop (group productization)
- Async tier review (buyer submits draft, gets written critique)
- Office hours (recurring group call)

**Access tiers**
- GitHub public repo (read-only, MIT)
- Private Discord/Slack community
- Direct DM access to maintainer
- Priority issue queue
- Early access to unreleased dimension packs

**Support levels**
- Community-only support
- Async ticket support (response SLA: 5 business days / 2 / 24h)
- Live debugging session
- Hands-on co-production (maintainer applies skill *for* the buyer)

**Customizations / variables**
- # of verticals supported (1 / 3 / unlimited)
- Memory backend (none / claude-mem / custom)
- Branding (Intelligent Clay branded / co-branded / white-label)
- Self-iteration GH Action (off / on / custom-tuned)
- Multi-seat (solo / team / org)
- Configurator export format (none / JSON / JSON+YAML+Typeform-ready)

---

## Part 2 — Configuration Architecture

| Dimension | What it controls | Levels | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Depth** | How much of the productization workflow is automated vs. assisted vs. done-for-you | Self-serve / Assisted / Done-with-you / Done-for-you | DFY requires live time blocks; Self-serve blocks live support |
| **Breadth** | How many verticals/dimension packs included | Core only / +1 vertical pack / All packs / All packs + custom | Custom pack requires Assisted or higher |
| **Support** | Response & format | Community / Async-ticket / Live-session / Embedded | Live-session requires ≥ Assisted depth |
| **Format** | Distribution & branding | OSS public / Private fork / Co-branded / White-label | White-label requires commercial license tier |
| **Memory & Tooling** | Persistence and automation surface | None / claude-mem default / Custom backend / Custom + GH Action tuning | Custom backend requires Live-session support |
| **Seats** | Who can use it inside the buyer's org | Solo / Team (≤10) / Org (unlimited) | Org seats require commercial license |
| **Configurator Export** *(domain-specific)* | What the buyer can hand to their own builder | None / JSON / JSON+YAML / Full embed kit (Typeform/Webflow templates) | Full embed kit requires ≥ +1 vertical pack |
| **Iteration Cadence** *(domain-specific)* | How often the skill updates flow to the buyer | Public release only / Quarterly digest / Monthly + changelog calls | Monthly requires Live-session support |

**Invalid combinations the configurator must block:**
- Self-serve depth + Live-session support
- White-label format + OSS-only license
- Custom memory backend without Live-session support
- Full embed kit export without at least one vertical pack
- Org seats on the free OSS tier
- Custom dimension pack request on Self-serve depth

---

## Part 3 — Three Tiers + Build-Your-Own

### Tier 1 — **Open** (free, MIT, current state)
- **Configuration:** Depth: Self-serve · Breadth: Core only · Support: Community · Format: OSS public · Memory: claude-mem default · Seats: Solo · Export: None · Cadence: Public release only
- **Positioning:** For solo operators who want to productize their own service using the public skill, with community support.
- **Pricing logic:** $0. Anchor: top-of-funnel; the skill itself is the lead magnet. Must remain genuinely useful standalone or the paid tiers lose credibility.

### Tier 2 — **Practitioner** *(flagship)* — `+$B/yr` (license) or `+$B` one-time
- **Configuration:** Depth: Assisted · Breadth: +1 vertical pack of choice · Support: Async-ticket (2-day SLA) + private community · Format: Private fork or co-branded · Memory: claude-mem default · Seats: Solo or Team (≤5) · Export: JSON · Cadence: Quarterly digest + changelog
- **Positioning:** For consultants and small agencies actively using the skill on client work — vertical-specific dimension pack, async expert review when stuck, and a configurator export they can drop into their own intake.
- **Pricing logic:** Anchored against (a) one productized client engagement the buyer closes faster, and (b) the labor cost of building dimension packs themselves (~40–80 hrs). Flagship discount vs. summing the parts at BYO rates.

### Tier 3 — **Studio** — `+$C/yr`
- **Configuration:** Depth: Done-with-you · Breadth: All packs + 1 custom pack · Support: Live-session (monthly) + 24h async SLA · Format: White-label · Memory: Custom backend supported · Seats: Org · Export: Full embed kit · Cadence: Monthly + changelog calls
- **Positioning:** For agencies and consultancies productizing at portfolio scale — white-labeled, custom dimension pack for their methodology, embedded in their own intake stack.
- **Pricing logic:** Anchored against an internal hire (productization lead, ~$120–180K loaded) or a McKinsey-style packaging engagement ($75K+). Studio comes in well below either.

### Build-Your-Own Framework

- **Base price:** Practitioner-equivalent license — `+$B` (anyone going beyond Open must start here)
- **Per-dimension upgrade pricing (placeholder tokens):**
  - Depth: Assisted → DWY: `+$d1` · DWY → DFY: `+$d2`
  - Breadth: +each vertical pack: `+$b1` · custom pack: `+$b2`
  - Support: Async → Live: `+$s1` · Live → Embedded: `+$s2`
  - Format: Co-branded → White-label: `+$f1`
  - Memory: claude-mem → Custom backend: `+$m1`
  - Seats: Solo/Team → Org: `+$st1`
  - Export: JSON → Full embed kit: `+$e1`
  - Cadence: Quarterly → Monthly: `+$c1`

- **Floor:** `+$B` (Practitioner license — no BYO below this; Open is the only free path)
- **Ceiling:** Any config crossing ~85% of Studio's price *or* requesting custom backend + custom pack + white-label simultaneously → auto-route to Studio (or to a custom enterprise quote)

- **Configurator dependencies (must enforce):**
  - White-label requires paid license (no White-label on Open)
  - Custom memory backend requires Live-session support
  - Custom dimension pack requires ≥ Done-with-you depth
  - Full embed kit export requires ≥ +1 vertical pack
  - Org seats locked to Studio or BYO-with-org-upgrade
  - Monthly cadence requires Live-session

- **Flagship-protection rule:** Summing BYO upgrades to reach Practitioner's exact config must price ~15–25% *above* `+$B`; summing to reach Studio's exact config must price ~20–30% above `+$C`. The flagships are always the rational discount. Re-price tokens until this holds.
